report_name: Healthcare Data Breaches at MN Epilepsy Group, Campbell University, and City of Middletown Expose Patient Info

threat_actors.name: SafePay
threat_actors.description: Ransomware group responsible for the attack on the City of Middletown in 2025, which compromised extensive personal and health information of over 20,000 individuals.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The SafePay ransomware group conducted a ransomware attack against the City of Middletown, encrypting systems to impact operations.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The SafePay group likely exfiltrated data from the City of Middletown for double extortion purposes before encrypting systems.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware actors, such as SafePay, commonly use tools like vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, preventing system recovery from backups.

attack_patterns.name: Clear Windows Event Logs
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware actors may use tools like wevtutil.exe to clear Windows Event Logs to cover their tracks and impede forensic investigation.

malware.name: SafePay Ransomware
malware.description: Ransomware used by the SafePay group to attack the City of Middletown. The malware was used to encrypt systems and exfiltrate data for double extortion.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: Mentioned as a ransomware-related process to monitor for, used to delete volume shadow copies to inhibit system recovery.

tools.name: wevtutil
tools.description: Mentioned as a ransomware-related process to monitor for, used to clear event logs to cover tracks.

identities.name: Minnesota Epilepsy Group
identities.description: A U.S. healthcare provider that suffered a network intrusion between March and April 2026, resulting in the exposure of patient PII and PHI.

identities.name: City of Middletown
identities.description: A city in New York that was the victim of a ransomware attack by the SafePay group in 2025, affecting over 20,000 individuals.

identities.name: Campbell University
identities.description: A university in North Carolina that experienced a data breach on a cloud storage platform in March-April 2026, affecting at least 500 individuals.

identities.name: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
identities.description: The U.S. federal agency whose Office for Civil Rights is responsible for enforcing HIPAA and investigating potential violations from data breaches in the healthcare sector.

infrastructure.name: SafePay Exfiltration Destination
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure used by the SafePay group to receive data exfiltrated from victims like the City of Middletown as part of their double extortion tactics.

indicators.name: Ransomware-Related Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware-Related Tool Execution
id: 7e8b1c2a-3d4e-4f5a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of tools commonly used by ransomware for defense evasion and inhibiting system recovery, such as deleting volume shadow copies or clearing event logs.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/minnesota-epilepsy-group-campbell-university-city-of-middletown-data-breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1070.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_vssadmin:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'delete shadows'
  selection_wevtutil:
    Image|endswith: '\wevtutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' cl '
      - ' clear-log '
  condition: 1 of them
indicators.description: Detects the execution of tools like vssadmin.exe or wevtutil.exe with commands typically used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery or clear evidence.
